qa_pairs.question: How do you establish an SSH tunnel from your desktop to a MySQL database server hosted on the Cedar cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To establish an SSH tunnel, you would use the command `ssh -L PORT:cedar-mysql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca:3306 someuser@fir.alliancecan.ca`, replacing `PORT` with your chosen local port number (which should not be bigger than 32768) and `someuser` with your account username.

qa_pairs.question: What command is used to connect to a PostgreSQL database server from your desktop after an SSH tunnel has been set up?
qa_pairs.answer: After setting up an SSH tunnel, you would run the command `psql -h 127.0.0.1 -p PORT -U <your username> -d <your database>` on your desktop to connect to the PostgreSQL database server.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the password for MySQL stored when connecting to a database server via an SSH tunnel?
qa_pairs.answer: The password for MySQL is stored in your `.my.cnf` file, which is located in your home directory on Fir.
